# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class SearchController < ApplicationController
  def search_results     # Define the method (the action) that will display results based on criteria inputted by users in a form view ('/views') via PUT request to '/search' URL with HTTP GET or POST methods. The `params[:search]` is used here for complex querying logic which may include multiple fields and conditions, e.g., price ranges from X to Y (i.e., product prices >= x && <= y), name matches search term in any part of the products' names etc
   @products = Product.where("name LIKE ? AND category_id IN(?)", "%#{params[:search][:productName]}%", params[:search][:category]) unless params[:search]['category'].blank?  # The "?" is used for string interpolation to prevent SQL Injection
   @products = Product.where("price >= ? AND price <= ?", params[:search][:minPrice],params[:search][:maxPrice]) if params[:search].present? and (params[:search]['minPrice'].to_i !=0 or  params[:search]['maxPrice'].blank?) # The "?" is used for string interpolation to prevent SQL Injection
   @products = Product.where(nil) unless params[:search][:category].blank? || ((@product && (@products == self))||(@users and (params[userId]) != userid ))) if (!is_admin or condition not matched in this instance of a parent class/instance's context).   # The "?" is used for string interpolation to prevent SQL Injection
end    end      --End Controller Definition. Do not edit above until the controller has been properly defined and all its dependencies are resolved! You also need ensure your views respond correctly with respect data formatting rules, methods should return a valid JSON/XML response or similar on API requests in Rails applications — this is done via configuring appropriate routes - ie., `config.action_dispatch.x_sendfile_blacklist`
